courses.title: Computer Fundamentals
courses.description: Introduction to computer architectures, hardware components, operating systems, and basic networking concepts.
outcomes: Understand basic organization of computer hardware and software systems.
Explain binary representations, logic gates, and Boolean algebra basics.
books: Introduction to Computers by Peter Norton
courses.reflection: This course provided a high-level map of the computing landscape. It sparked my interest in how high-level code interacts with physical gates.

courses.title: Mathematics-I (Differential & Integral Calculus)
courses.description: Fundamentals of functions, limits, continuity, differentiation, integration, and their physical applications.
outcomes: Apply derivatives to solve optimization and rate-of-change problems.
Evaluate definite and indefinite integrals for area and volume calculations.
courses.reflection: Calculus built the mathematical analytical mindset needed to understand algorithm complexity curves and machine learning principles later.

courses.title: Structured Programming Language
courses.description: Introduction to structured programming concepts using the C language, covering loops, arrays, structures, pointers, and files.
outcomes: Deconstruct complex tasks into functional sub-modules in C.
Manage manual memory using pointers, references, and structures.
books: Programming in ANSI C by E. Balagurusamy
courses.reflection: My first programming language! Mastering pointers in C was challenging but gave me a deep understanding of memory address layouts.

courses.title: Object Oriented Programming Language
courses.description: Object-oriented software development using C++/Java, covering classes, polymorphism, inheritance, templates, and exception handling.
outcomes: Apply encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism to model real-world systems.
Write clean modular OOP architectures using abstract classes and interfaces.
books: Java: The Complete Reference by Herbert Schildt
courses.reflection: Transitioning from C to C++ and Java opened my eyes to design patterns and the beauty of modular, reusable code architectures.

courses.title: Data Structures
courses.description: Concepts of linear and non-linear data structures: stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, graphs, and hash tables.
outcomes: Implement and optimize core data structures in standard programming languages.
Compare memory-time trade-offs of lists, hash maps, and binary search trees.
books: Data Structures and Algorithms Made Easy by Narasimha Karumanchi
courses.reflection: Data structures showed me how raw bytes can be organized to speed up search and retrieval. This is where I started competitive programming.

courses.title: Database Management Systems
courses.description: Relational database design, normalization (1NF-BCNF), SQL querying, transaction management (ACID properties), and indexing.
outcomes: Model database architectures using ER diagrams and relational mappings.
Optimize database structures using normalization and query indexes.
books: Database System Concepts by Silberschatz, Korth & Sudarshan
courses.reflection: This course was critical. I built a library inventory database and normalization rules became second nature. I later connected this learning to AWS RDS.

courses.title: Algorithms
courses.description: Analysis of algorithm complexities, sorting, searching, greedy algorithms, dynamic programming, and graph algorithms.
outcomes: Analyze time and space complexities of algorithms using asymptotic notations.
Design optimal solutions using Dynamic Programming, Greedy approaches, and Graph traversals.
courses.reflection: CLRS is the bible of algorithms. Tackling dynamic programming problems sharpened my logical reasoning, heavily improving my CP stats.

courses.title: Operating Systems
courses.description: OS principles including process management, CPU scheduling, thread synchronization, deadlocks, memory management, and file systems.
outcomes: Evaluate process scheduling and resource allocation algorithms.
Write multi-threaded routines and resolve thread synchronization deadlocks.
books: Operating System Concepts by Avi Silberschatz
courses.reflection: Writing thread managers and understanding paging/virtual memory helped me understand the inner workings of cloud computing instances like EC2.

courses.title: Web Engineering
courses.description: Modern web development paradigms, client-server architectures, frontend frameworks, API design, and web security.
outcomes: Construct responsive, semantic layouts using HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript.
Build secure, RESTful backend APIs with relational and document databases.
books: Web Engineering: A Practitioner's Approach by Roger Pressman
courses.reflection: This course aligned perfectly with my Fiverr freelance work. I applied responsive web design, REST design, and basic security modules.

courses.title: Computer Networks
courses.description: Layered architecture (OSI vs TCP/IP), physical media, routing algorithms, routing protocols, flow control, and IP subnetting.
outcomes: Perform IP subnetting and configure logical network topologies.
Explain routing protocols (OSPF, BGP, RIP) and TCP transmission mechanics.
books: Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach by Kurose & Ross
courses.reflection: Understanding the TCP/IP stack made AWS VPC configurations easy. Subnetting, gate routing, and NATs translated directly to cloud VPC setups.

courses.title: Cloud Computing
courses.description: Cloud architectures, virtualization, containerization, serverless computing, and AWS services ecosystem.
outcomes: Architect scalable, fault-tolerant infrastructures using Amazon Web Services (AWS).
Implement automated cloud deployments and cost-efficient monitoring.
books: Cloud Computing Architecture by AWS Technical Guides
courses.reflection: This course was the launching pad for my AWS Cloud Journey. I built labs using EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, and DynamoDB. I got inspired to pursue the AWS Cloud Practitioner roadmap.

courses.title: UI/UX Design
courses.description: User-centered design principles, wireframing, user persona research, prototyping, design systems, and Figma mechanics.
outcomes: Conduct structured UX research, empathy mapping, and usability testing.
Create high-fidelity interactive prototypes and design systems in Figma.
books: Don't Make Me Think by Steve Krug
courses.reflection: This course validated my Fiverr design experience. I studied the cognitive principles of design, wireframing, and interactive system flows.

courses.title: Capstone Thesis & Project
courses.description: Major research and development capstone demonstrating CSE competency, team leadership, architectural design, and implementation.
outcomes: Coordinated team tasks, scheduled milestones, and presented progress updates.
Developed and deployed a complex software product incorporating design and cloud.
courses.reflection: As the Team Leader for our capstone project, I combined my engineering, UI/UX design, and AWS cloud hosting skills to direct my team to a successful defense!
